entries.title: Trump's Lebanon ceasefire takes Israel by surprise
entries.snippet: Few Israelis see this truce as a way out of the conflict with Hezbollah, the BBC's Lucy Williamson writes.

entries.title: Singer D4vd arrested on suspicion of murdering teenage girl
entries.snippet: The remains of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez were found in the singer's car last year.

entries.title: Finance ministers and top bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model
entries.snippet: Experts say Mythos potentially has an unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cyber-security weaknesses.

entries.title: 'I was tortured and lost my hand' - one student's struggle to get an education in Nigeria
entries.snippet: The BBC speaks to a student who pushed for his toe print to be taken to verify his identity.

entries.title: Iranian footballers say Australia has given them 'hope' for safe future
entries.snippet: The duo had sought aslyum after their football team did not sing the national anthem during a match.

entries.title: Runaway wolf finally captured after nine-day search in South Korea
entries.snippet: The search has been marked by twists and turns, gripping South Korea and even inspiring a meme coin.

entries.title: Naples bank robbers hold 25 people hostage then vanish through tunnel
entries.snippet: The armed men reportedly evaded capture by escaping through the city's sewer system.

entries.title: Three charged with arson on Persian media offices in London
entries.snippet: Two teenagers and a 21-year-old man are due in court charged with arson with intent to endanger life.

entries.title: Pope criticises 'tyrants' who spend billions on wars after Trump spat
entries.snippet: The comments follow a high-profile spat with US President Donald Trump, who called the Pope weak on crime.

entries.title: Turkish police detain 162 people over online posts about school shootings
entries.snippet: At least 16 people were injured in a shooting at a high school on Tuesday, before another nine were killed in a separate school shooting on Wednesday.

entries.title: Brazil's former spy chief released from ICE detention
entries.snippet: Bolsonaro ally Alexandre Ramagem was stopped by immigration agents in Orlando, Florida on Monday.

entries.title: Russia launches deadliest aerial attack in months, killing 18 in Ukraine
entries.snippet: A Ukrainian drone attack killed two people in Russia, Moscow says, after an Orthodox Easter truce.

entries.title: Lawmakers clash with RFK Jr as he shifts focus away from vaccines
entries.snippet: During a three-hour hearing, the US health secretary tried to focus on chronic disease while being pressed on vaccines.

entries.title: Rescuers to use air cushions in latest effort to save stranded whale
entries.snippet: "Timmy" has been stranded in the Baltic Sea for weeks despite several attempts to free the ailing animal.

entries.title: Ex-Virginia Lt. Governor kills wife and himself in murder-suicide, police say
entries.snippet: Investigators say Justin Fairfax shot his wife, Cerina, multiple times before turning the gun on himself.

entries.title: South African opposition figure Malema sentenced to five years in prison for firing a gun
entries.snippet: Malema is appealing against the decision, which means he is not going to prison immediately.

entries.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
entries.snippet: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

entries.title: Avengers reassemble and Top Gun flies back - Hollywood studios preview their new movies
entries.snippet: Some of the most hotly anticipated new films of the next couple of years are previewed at CinemaCon.

entries.title: School shootings a new trauma for Turkey as nation mourns
entries.snippet: An expert tells the BBC the attack in Kahramanmaras was a tragedy but "not a surprise".

entries.title: 'How does one survive?': Factory protests expose strain in India's industrial system
entries.snippet: Workers in some north Indian cities have been protesting, demanding better pay and working conditions.

entries.title: Leaders urge for restraint as 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire takes effect
entries.snippet: Hezbollah, the Iran-backed militant group, acknowledged the ceasefire, but did not say whether it would abide by it and urged people displaced by fighting in Lebanon to refrain from heading home.

entries.title: Israel starts a tense ceasefire in Lebanon. And, Trump nominates a new CDC director.
entries.snippet: A 10-day ceasefire to pause fighting between Israel and Hezb

entries.title: Who says they have no fear of the Trump administration? The quiz knows
entries.snippet: Also: If you know what Eric Swalwell looks like, you'll get at least one question correct.

entries.title: Do less, ask for more: How to make life easier as a working parent
entries.snippet: Going back to work after having a baby can be overwhelming. You're juggling all the emotions of being a new parent while getting up to speed at your job. Tips to help you make a smooth transition.

entries.title: The Labor Department wants to teach you to use AI more. Here's what we found
entries.snippet: The short course provides solid basics for using AI. But it also misidentifies AI products, links out to bad advice and raises ethical concerns about the products it promotes

entries.title: Families left reeling after hospitals in blue states drop transgender care for youth
entries.snippet: Massachusetts passed laws and joined lawsuits to protect access to gender-affirming care for minors. But faced with the Trump administration's threats, some hospitals voluntarily stopped care.

entries.title: Logjam of U.S. immigration applications puts millions at greater risk of deportation
entries.snippet: An NPR analysis shows how immigrants' attempts to live or work legally in the U.S. are caught in a bureaucratic morass.

entries.title: Human rights groups raise alarm over fate of Salvadorans deported from U.S.
entries.snippet: Migrants deported from the U.S. routinely disappear into El Salvador's prisons the moment they land or in the weeks that follow. Many remain incommunicado from family and lawyers for years.

entries.title: Morning news brief
entries.snippet: Israel and Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire, U.S. military officials say the blockade of Iranian ports and ceasefire is holding, Trump nominates former Coast Guard doctor as CDC chief.

entries.title: Woman remembers her childhood babysitter -- Prince
entries.snippet: For StoryCorps, a family that lived near Prince in Minnesota remembers the artist in his early years -- as their babysitter.

entries.title: Ex-NBA player Damon Jones expected to make 1st guilty plea in gambling sweep
entries.snippet: Jones had previously pleaded not guilty to separate indictments charging him with profiting from rigged poker games and providing sports bettors with non-public information about injuries to stars LeBron James and Anthony Davis.

entries.title: What lagging jet fuel supplies could mean for airlines and travelers
entries.snippet: A looming jet fuel shortage in Europe and Asia sparked by the Iran war and the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz could further upend world travel within weeks if oil doesn't start flowing again soon — meaning higher airfares and flight cancellations as the summer travel s

entries.title: Duffy withholds federal funding from New York over immigrant trucker licenses dispute
entries.snippet: New York will lose more than $73.5 million in federal money because the Transportation Department said Thursday that state has refused to revoke nearly 33,000 questionable commercial driver's licenses for immigrants since an audit uncovered problems last year.

entries.title: House rejects effort to withdraw U.S. forces from Iran war as GOP lawmakers stick with Trump
entries.snippet: The House rejected a resolution Thursday requiring President Donald Trump to withdraw U.S. forces from the war with Iran unless Congress authorizes military action. It was the latest such vote that fell short of passage as Republicans largely continue to support Trump's operation

entries.title: Israel agrees to 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon as U.S. pushes for broader peace deal
entries.snippet: President Trump announced a ceasefire deal that would suspend fighting between Israel and Hezbollah for ten days. Hezbollah has not said whether it will abide by the ceasefire. Trump also says leaders from Israel and Lebanon are expected to meet soon in hopes of reaching a broade

entries.title: Why the Israeli and Lebanese governments accepted a ceasefire – and will Hezbollah abide?
entries.snippet: For insights on the announced ceasefire by the U.S., Israel and Lebanon, Amna Nawaz spoke with author and journalist Kim Ghattas. Her recent book, "Black Wave," is about the rivalry between Saudi Arabia and Iran, and she is now a visiting professor at Dartmouth College.

entries.title: UN official warns Strait of Hormuz dispute is disrupting global food supply
entries.snippet: To discuss the global stakes of the impasse in the Strait of Hormuz and its impact on deepening humanitarian crises, Geoff Bennett spoke with Jorge Moreira da Silva, the executive director of the UN's Office for Project Services and head of the UN task force on the strait.

entries.title: News Wrap: Pope warns of 'tyrants' spending billions on wars
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Thursday, Pope Leo is warning of a world "ravaged by a handful of tyrants" who spend billions on war, the Senate voted to lift a federal ban on mining upstream from Minnesota's Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness and police say former Virginia Lt. Gov. Justin F

entries.title: How much of Project 2025 has Trump enacted?
entries.snippet: White House budget director Russ Vought has been key to implementing the Trump agenda. But before joining the administration, he was a central figure in drafting Project 2025, the controversial policy playbook by the Heritage Foundation that suggested large-scale changes a Republ

entries.title: Hampshire College closure highlights financial strain on small liberal arts schools
entries.snippet: After years of financial decline, Hampshire College, a liberal arts college in Massachusetts, has announced it will close at the end of the year. But the college is hardly alone. A new estimate projects that nearly 450 of the nation's 1,700 private, nonprofit colleges and univers

entries.title: Tracking the Trump family's business deals and profits in his 2nd term
entries.snippet: In his second administration, President Trump's family members, including his son-in-law Jared Kushner, and sons Eric and Don Jr., are expanding their business ventures, earning hundreds of millions of dollars and prompting fresh concerns about influence peddling and conflicts of

entries.title: Erica Schwartz, former deputy surgeon general, nominated to be next CDC director by Trump
entries.snippet: The Atlanta-based CDC, which is charged with protecting Americans from preventable health threats, has been in turmoil since Trump returned to office more than a year ago, with a succession of mostly temporary leaders.

entries.title: Dave Chappelle on investing in his Ohio town and backing its local public radio station
entries.snippet: For more than 25 years, comedian Dave Chappelle has called the small village of Yellow Springs, Ohio, home. Amna Nawaz traveled there to meet with Chappelle and understand why he's invested millions of dollars into this community, and why he believes the local public media statio

entries.title: WATCH: Trump 'OK' with public Epstein survivor hearings
entries.snippet: "I'm OK with it," he told PBS News' Liz Landers during a media availability outside the White House. "I think we've had a lot of public hearings. I'm OK with it, but I understand that the women didn't want to go under oath."

entries.title: Cuba's president says island does not wish for U.S. aggression but ready to fight if necessary
entries.snippet: Earlier this week, Trump said his administration could focus on Cuba after the war in Iran ends.

entries.title: WATCH: Trump says the pope has to understand that Iran cannot have a nuclear weapon
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump said Thursday that he has "to do what's right" as he pushes back against Pope Leo XIV on Iran war.

entries.title: Judge allows 'national security' work to proceed at White House ballroom construction site
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump railed against a federal judge's decision on Thursday that continues to block above-ground construction of a $400 million White House ballroom, allowing only below-ground work on a bunker and other "national security facilities" at the site.

entries.title: WATCH: Vought won't say how much Trump has spent on Iran war so far
entries.snippet: Russell Vought, director of the Office of Management and Budget, declined to tell lawmakers on the Senate Budget Committee in a hearing Thursday how much the federal government has spent on the Iran war so far. Watch the clip in the video player above. "We don't have that figures

entries.title: WATCH: OMB chief Vought says Homeland Security is 'disintegrating' during shutdown
entries.snippet: Russell Vought, director of the Office of Management and Budget, told lawmakers in a hearing Thursday that the Department of Homeland Security is "disintegrating" as it remains unfunded by Congress.

entries.title: WATCH: Trump attends Tax Day roundtable in Las Vegas as he touts 'no tax on tips' policy
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump is betting that the tax cuts he signed into law last year will resonate with voters in Las Vegas, where he highlighted his tax breaks for tipped workers in an appeal targeted at this year's midterm elections.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Chess: Uzbekistan's new star shows Asia's continued rise
entries.snippet: Javokhir Sindarov of Uzbekistan has earned the right to challenge India's Dommaraju Gukesh for the world title. Both men are under 21 and from Asia, underlining an ongoing demographic shift in top level chess.

entries.title: Myanmar frees over 4,000 prisoners in annual tradition
entries.snippet: Myanmar's new president says he wants stability and reconciliation in a nation torn apart by a military coup. Those to be released include former President Win Myint.

entries.title: Nueva Germania: The failed 'Aryan project' in Paraguay
entries.snippet: A new home for the "Aryan race." That was what German emigrants envisioned when they founded Nueva Germania in Paraguay in 1886. It failed back then but a version of the town still exists today.

entries.title: Germany news: Far-right AfD ahead of Merz's conservatives
entries.snippet: The far-right Alternative for Germany has nudged ahead of its political rivals in a new poll, the most recent of several that it has topped. Meanwhile, a last-ditch attempt to save whale Timmy is underway. Follow DW.

entries.title: Nuba Mountains, a fragile refuge on Sudan's front line
entries.snippet: Squeezed between Sudan and South Sudan, the self-governed Nuba Mountains are grappling with complex war dynamics while hosting hundreds of thousands of refugees they are scarcely able to support.

entries.title: Overshadowed by Iran, Gaza stuck between war and peace
entries.snippet: With the war in Iran, the conflict in Gaza has faded from international attention. Despite a six-month ceasefire, efforts to end the conflict have stalled.

entries.title: India: Parliament votes on women's quota, delimitation amid opposition row
entries.snippet: India's Modi government is eyeing a passage of key bills that could overhaul Parliament seats and pave the way for a women's quota. The opposition has reservations, casting doubt on the intentions behind the push.

entries.title: US military in Libya: Pursuing unity, or pressuring Russia?
entries.snippet: For the first time, Libya is hosting the Flintlock military exercise and soldiers from both sides of the divided country are taking part. It's due to security concerns, economic interests and competition with Russia.

entries.title: Will Bulgaria's election change the country's course?
entries.snippet: Bulgarians will vote in their eighth parliamentary election in five years on April 19. Former President Rumen Radev is projected to win. Radev, who has a record of pro-Russian stances, is pledging to fight corruption.

entries.title: Roberts-Smith gets bail in Australia over Afghan war crimes
entries.snippet: Australia’s most decorated living soldier has been granted bail after being charged with war crimes in Afghanistan. The case is among the highest-profile in the country.

entries.title: IMF, World Bank restore relations with Venezuela
entries.snippet: The IMF and World Bank have resumed formal engagement with Venezuela. The move further legitimizes the Latin American country's interim government and paves the way for financial support.

entries.title: Iran war: Temporary Israel-Lebanon ceasefire takes effect
entries.snippet: As a 10-day ceasefire deal between Lebanon and Israel took effect, the Israeli military said its forces are going to remain in southern Lebanon. DW has the latest.

entries.title: Cuban president vows defense of island if US invades
entries.snippet: Cuba's president has vowed the Caribbean island state will defend itself if the US launches a military assault. US President Trump has repeatedly threatened to invade.

entries.title: Several killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine, many more injured
entries.snippet: A 12-year-old was among those killed as Russian missile and drone attacks hit Ukrainian cities of Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro.

entries.title: 10-day ceasefire between Israel, Lebanon goes into effect
entries.snippet: The temporary ceasefire came after US President Trump spoke with Lebanese President Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu. Hezbollah says its commitment to the truce depends on Israel stopping attacks.

entries.title: Pope Leo excoriates 'tyrants' waging war instead of peace
entries.snippet: Pope Leo XIV has admonished those waging war in the name of god and slammed "tyrants" destroying the world and its resources. The pontiff has stepped up his criticism of senseless violence, angering US President Trump.

entries.title: The immense cost of Iran's nuclear program
entries.snippet: Iran insists that its nuclear program is for peaceful, civilian purposes, including energy production. But the figures and statistics paint a different picture.

entries.title: Why India's SMEs are suffering from Hormuz Strait crisis
entries.snippet: Since the blockade of the vital shipping route, spice hub Kerala and ceramics manufacturing center Morbi are two of regional sites in India affected. Can anything be done to support small-scale traders there?

entries.title: Senegal: The Goethe-Institut opens its new building in Dakar
entries.snippet: The Goethe-Institut's new building in Dakar champions sustainable architecture, made from the earth. Star architect Francis Kere has blended local materials with contemporary design.
